name: Big Pine Key Park
description: Big Pine Key Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Big Pine Key, FL. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free.

name: Blue Heron Community Park
description: Blue Heron Community Park is a place to play pickleball in Big Pine Key, FL. There are 6 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like lights. The courts are free.

name: Buttonwood Bay Condominium
description: Buttonwood Bay Condominium is a place to play pickleball in Key Largo, FL. There are 5 outdoor asphalt courts. Tape lines have been laid, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are private.

name: Club Duck Key
description: Come play pickleball at Club Duck Key in Duck Key, FL! There is 1 outdoor asphalt court. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include restrooms and water. A membership is required to play.
fees: Members only — See clubduckkey.com for details

name: Founders Park
description: Come play pickleball at Founders Park in Islamorada, FL! There are 4 hard courts. 3 are outdoors and 1 is indoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include lights and restrooms. The courts can be reserved. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Gib Reese Park
description: Gib Reese Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Islamorada, FL. There are 3 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts cannot be reserved. Players have access to restrooms. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Hawks Cay Resort
description: Hawks Cay Resort is a place to play pickleball in Marathon, FL. There are 8 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Reservations are available. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include restrooms and trainers or lessons.

name: Health & Tennis Club At Ocean Reef
description: Health & Tennis Club At Ocean Reef is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Key Largo, FL. There are 17 outdoor clay and hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. Amenities include restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, and a pro shop or equipment store. Reservations are available.

name: Higgs Beach Park Tennis Courts
description: Higgs Beach Park Tennis Courts is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Key West, FL. There are 12 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts can be reserved. Amenities include restrooms, water, trainers or lessons, and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are free.

name: Key Colony Beach Pickleball
description: Come play pickleball at Key Colony Beach Pickleball in Key Colony Beach, FL! There are 10 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms and water. A membership is required to play. Reservations are not available.
fees: Members only — Visitor Pass  or Membership Required Nov 1-April 30

name: Key Largo Community Park
description: Come play pickleball at Key Largo Community Park in Key Largo, FL! There are 10 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms and water. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are free.

name: Marathon Community Park
description: Marathon Community Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Marathon, FL. There are 5 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights.

name: Mariners Club
description: In order to reserve the indoor court, you must be staying as a guest at Mariners Club. And you must sign up sign a waiver and pay for the court-on the court reserve app. The outside courts are first come, first serve.
fees: Members only — Indoor Court fees are $40.00 per hour by Court Reserve and the Outdoor courts are part of the amenities at Mariners club

name: Outdoor Resorts At Long Key
description: Outdoor Resorts At Long Key is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Layton, FL. There is 1 outdoor court. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Sobek Pickleball
description: Come play pickleball at Sobek Pickleball in Key West, FL! There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. You can reserve the courts. The facility includes amenities like lights, restrooms, and trainers or lessons.

name: Sunshine Key Rv Resort
description: Sunshine Key Rv Resort is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Big Pine Key, FL. There are 2 outdoor courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. A one-time fee is required to play. Reservations are available.
